name: SOL Inventory Reset Reclaim v1
thesis: SOL 4h is the best-fit basket lane for testing inventory-reset continuation after a flush-and-reclaim rather than static oversold bounce logic. The edge should come from reclaim-after-flush behavior with broader participation thresholds and staged management.
invalidation_condition: Abort if this remains too sparse, structurally contradictory, or cannot produce enough blind-window trades after costs.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Sharp flush resets inventory, price reclaims value, and continuation follows because the panic move was over-extended.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: In transition conditions, forced inventory reset can create a cleaner second-leg move than raw oversold bounce logic.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: SOL 4h fits better than 1h because the inventory reset should be meaningful enough to create multi-bar continuation rather than noise-only rebounds.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If the market remains pure chop instead of transition, reclaim moves may only bounce and fade rather than continue.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If some trades appear but density is weak, reduce reclaim distance requirements before changing management.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; reclaim and directional confirmation align after the flush
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by broad flush threshold and simple qualifier stack

long: Trail remaining 25% by 0.7 * ATR_14 after first partial
short: Trail remaining 25% by 0.7 * ATR_14 after first partial
